text: Okay, this is the operating process of this reactor.
This reactor uses dual gas configuration with no middle electrolyte chamber
and only one EM membrane between the cathode and the anode.
First, I will describe the gas bed configuration.
The anode gas is hydrogen with a flow rate of 20 SACM.
The cathode feed gas is carbon monoxide with a flow rate of 10 SACM.
In the electrolyte chemical setup, the working electrode is connected to the cathode.
Also, the counter electrode and the reference electrode are connected to the anode side.
During the operation, we did not observe any cathode flooding or anode flooding.
There is no visible liquid droplets at the cathode or anode gas gap.
It indicates that the cathode and anode gas passivate remains open and a little dry.
This is an important difference from the previous liquid middle chamber design
where liquid conservation and cathode flooding were observed.
By removing the middle electrolyte chamber, this reactor reduces the risk of liquid accumulation and pressure imbalance.
The main goal of this test is to evaluate whether this compact dual gas CRAM HVAC configuration can maintain stable operation without cathode flooding.
